title: lithocarpus costatus var. typica
intro: Lithocarpus costatus var. typica, also known as the Pacific tan oak, is a tree species native to the Pacific coast of North America, characterized by its broad leaves and rounded crown. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of tanins and other bioactive compounds. The bark and leaves of the Pacific tan oak have been used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Lithocarpus costatus var. typica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
